---
description: 'Analyze the latest Stryker mutation report and open a GitHub issue with triaged recommendations for surviving mutants worth killing.'
tools: ['read_file', 'search_files', 'run_in_terminal']
---

# Improve Mutation Score — Issue Recommendations

You are running in **GitHub Copilot CLI inside a GitHub Actions workflow**. The
mutation testing job has just finished and produced a Stryker report. Your job is
to read that report, decide which surviving mutants are genuinely worth killing,
and **open a single GitHub issue** with concrete recommendations.

You **must not** modify source or test files in this run. Open an issue only.

---

## Step 1 — Locate the mutation report

The report is at the path given in the **Context Paths** section below
(typically `vscode-extension/reports/mutation/mutation.json`). If the file does
not exist or has zero mutants, exit cleanly without opening an issue and print:

```
NO_REPORT — nothing to recommend.
```

---

## Step 2 — Parse the report

The Stryker JSON has the structure:

```json
{
  "files": {
    "out/src/foo.js": {
      "mutants": [
        { "id": "...", "mutatorName": "...", "replacement": "...",
          "location": { "start": { "line": N, "column": N }, "end": { ... } },
          "status": "Survived", "description": "..." }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

Compute:
- `total`, `killed`, `survived`, `timedOut`, `noCoverage`
- `score = killed / (killed + survived + timedOut) * 100`

Map compiled JS paths back to TypeScript sources:

| Compiled path | TypeScript source |
|---|---|
| `out/src/tokenEstimation.js` | `vscode-extension/src/tokenEstimation.ts` |
| `out/src/sessionParser.js` | `vscode-extension/src/sessionParser.ts` |
| `out/src/workspaceHelpers.js` | `vscode-extension/src/workspaceHelpers.ts` |
| `out/src/claudecode.js` | `vscode-extension/src/claudecode.ts` |
| `out/src/utils/dayKeys.js` | `vscode-extension/src/utils/dayKeys.ts` |
| `out/src/utils/errors.js` | `vscode-extension/src/utils/errors.ts` |
| `out/src/utils/html.js` | `vscode-extension/src/utils/html.ts` |

For unmapped paths, infer the TypeScript path by replacing `out/` with `vscode-extension/` and `.js` with `.ts`.

---

## Step 3 — Triage surviving mutants

For each `Survived` mutant, read a few lines of TypeScript context around its
location and classify it as one of:

- ✅ **Worth killing** — reveals a real coverage gap (boundary, formatting,
  guard branch, flippable boolean, exact string contract).
- ⚠️ **Marginal** — defensive checks on validated data, internal details with
  no observable effect.
- 🚫 **Skip** — equivalent mutants, log/error string wording, intentionally
  untestable code (VS Code API, real FS I/O in `extension.ts`).

Limit the issue to the **top ~15 worth-killing** mutants. Group by source file.

---

## Step 4 — Deduplicate against existing issues

Before creating a new issue, search open issues with the `mutation-testing`
label:

```bash
gh issue list --label mutation-testing --state open --json number,title,body --limit 20
```

If an open issue already lists the same top mutants (same file + line + mutator
for ≥ 3 of your top 5 picks), **do not open a duplicate**. Instead, post a brief
comment on that issue noting today's score and that the top mutants still
survive, then exit.

---

## Step 5 — Create the issue

Use `gh issue create`. Title:

```
Mutation testing: <N> surviving mutants worth killing (score <X.X>%)
```

Body (Markdown):

```
> Auto-generated from the daily mutation testing run.
> Workflow run: <link to ${GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/actions/runs/${GITHUB_RUN_ID}>

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| 📊 Score | X.X% |
| ✅ Killed | … |
| ⚠️ Survived | … |
| 🕐 Timeout | … |
| 🙈 NoCoverage | … |
| Σ Total | … |

## Recommended mutants to kill

### `vscode-extension/src/<file>.ts`

- **Line N — `<mutatorName>`**: `<description>`
  - Why it matters: <one sentence>
  - Suggested test: <one sentence describing the assertion that would kill it>

(repeat per mutant, grouped by file)

## Skipped categories

Brief note on what was deliberately not included (equivalent mutants, log strings, etc.).
```

Apply labels `mutation-testing` and `autogenerated`. If those labels don't
exist, create them first with `gh label create` (idempotent — ignore "already
exists" errors).

---

## Constraints

- **Do not** edit any source or test files. Issue creation only.
- **Do not** open more than one issue per run.
- Be concise: the issue should be skimmable. Prefer 10–15 high-quality
  recommendations over 50 mediocre ones.
- If `gh` commands fail due to auth/permissions, print the error and exit
  non-zero so the workflow surfaces it — do **not** silently swallow failures.
- Honor the same triage guardrails from the interactive `Improve Mutation
  Score` agent: prefer `assert.strictEqual`-style suggestions, avoid trivial
  mocks, ignore equivalent mutants.
